heading: Increased military assistance for the Government of Ukraine
label: 8925 Increased military assistance for the Government of Ukraine

Delegation of Authority Under the Ukraine Freedom Support Act of 2014Memorandum of President of the United States, , 80 F.R. 12071, provided:Feb. 19, 2015Memorandum for the Secretary of StateBy the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including , United States Code, I hereby order as follows:section 301 of title 3I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State the authority to prepare and submit to the Congress the reports and strategies required by subsections 6(b), 7(d), 9(c), and 10(c) of the Ukraine Freedom Support Act of 2014 () (the “Act”).Public Law 113–272Any reference in this memorandum to the Act shall be deemed to be a reference to any future Act that is the same or substantially the same as such provision.Barack Obama.

Delegation of Authority Under the Ukraine Freedom Support Act of 2014Memorandum of President of the United States, , 80 F.R. 12071, provided:Feb. 19, 2015Memorandum for the Secretary of StateBy the authority vested in me as President by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including , United States Code, I hereby order as follows:section 301 of title 3I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State the authority to prepare and submit to the Congress the reports and strategies required by subsections 6(b), 7(d), 9(c), and 10(c) of the Ukraine Freedom Support Act of 2014 () (the “Act”).Public Law 113–272Any reference in this memorandum to the Act shall be deemed to be a reference to any future Act that is the same or substantially the same as such provision.Barack Obama.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The President is authorized to provide defense articles, defense services, and training to the Government of Ukraine for the purpose of countering offensive weapons and reestablishing the sovereignty and territorial integrity of Ukraine, including anti-tank and anti-armor weapons, crew weapons and ammunition, counter-artillery radars to identify and target artillery batteries, fire control, range finder, and optical and guidance and control equipment, tactical troop-operated surveillance drones, and secure command and communications equipment, pursuant to the provisions of the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.), the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.), and other relevant provisions of law.22 U.S.C. 275122 U.S.C. 2151
the Committee on Foreign Relations, the Committee on Appropriations, and the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate; and
the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Appropriations, and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary of State $100,000,000 for fiscal year 2015, $125,000,000 for fiscal year 2016, and $125,000,000 for fiscal year 2017 to carry out activities under this section.

children.heading: Availability of amounts
children.label: 2 Availability of amounts
children.text: Amounts authorized to be appropriated pursuant to paragraph (1) shall remain available for obligation and expenditure through the end of fiscal year 2018.

children.heading: Authority for the use of funds
children.label: d Authority for the use of funds
children.text: The funds made available pursuant to subsection (c) for provision of defense articles, defense services, and training may be used to procure such articles, services, and training from the United States Government or other appropriate sources.

children.heading: Protection of civilians
children.label: e Protection of civilians
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the Government of Ukraine should take all appropriate steps to protect civilians.
